The text mentions that experts from the IOC were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. It then states that the IOC required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test, and the Shanghai Mint even raised that to 3 meters. The medals can survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks. So, the key point here is the concern about fragility. The IOC was worried that the jade rings might be too fragile and could break easily. Let's look at the answer choices again: A) the medals will be worth less – The text doesn't mention anything about the monetary value of the medals being a concern for the IOC.
